description: Asserting rigid exact rules (zero nulls, exact counts) that fire on benign variation, training the team to ignore alerts.
body: Failure mode: checks demand exact values that real-world data never sustains, so they fire constantly, get muted or routed to a dead channel, and a genuine incident is then ignored along with the noise. Detection signals: a flood of quality alerts that are routinely dismissed; thresholds set to absolutes with no historical basis; the channel is muted. Do not set zero-tolerance rules on naturally variable data. Safer fallback: calibrate thresholds from historical distributions, reserve absolutes for true invariants like key uniqueness, and verify a low false-positive rate before paging.
